content.explanation:
**Would + Ought To + Dare — Advanced Modals:**

**WOULD (3 main uses):**

1. **Polite Requests:**
🇬🇧 **Would** you please send the report? (बेहद polite)

2. **Past Habits (recurring past actions):**
🇮🇳 जब मैं छोटा था, तो रोज़ football खेलता था।
🇬🇧 When I was young, I **would** play football every day.

3. **Conditional (with "if" or imaginary):**
🇮🇳 मैं help करता, अगर मेरे पास time होता।
🇬🇧 I **would** help if I had time.

---

**OUGHT TO (moral duty — stronger than should):**
🇮🇳 तुम्हें honest होना चाहिए।
🇬🇧 You **ought to** be honest.

Structure: Subject + **ought to** + V1
Negative: Subject + **ought not to** / **oughtn't to** + V1

---

**DARE (हिम्मत करना / challenge):**
🇮🇳 वह सच बोलने की हिम्मत रखती है।
🇬🇧 She **dares to** speak the truth.

Negative: I **dare not** / **don't dare to** speak.
Challenge: **How dare** you say that!
Question: **Dare** she ask? (formal/literary)
rules: Would + V1 = polite request, past habit, conditional
'Would' is past form of 'will' — but often used independently
Ought to + V1 = moral obligation (like should, but stronger)
Ought not to + V1 = negative duty
Dare to + V1 = to have courage to do something
'How dare you!' = expression of shock/anger (not a question)
'Dare not' (modal use) = very formal literary English
I wouldn't do that = strong negative advice
content.memoryTrick: **Would = Soft Cushion 🛋️** — Would request पर एक soft cushion है — Will से ज़्यादा polite। **Ought = Moral Compass 🧭** — Ought to अंदर से moral direction देता है। **Dare = Lion Heart 🦁** — Dare = हिम्मत जो lion की होती है।

examples.hindi: क्या आप please window बंद कर देंगे?
examples.english: Would you please close the window?

examples.hindi: बचपन में वह रोज़ library जाता था।
examples.english: He would go to the library every day as a child.

examples.hindi: तुम्हें अपने parents को respect करना चाहिए।
examples.english: You ought to respect your parents.

examples.hindi: उसे ऐसा नहीं करना चाहिए था।
examples.english: He ought not to have done that.

examples.hindi: वह अकेले stage पर जाने की हिम्मत रखता है।
examples.english: He dares to go on stage alone.

examples.hindi: तुम्हारी यह हिम्मत कैसे हुई ऐसा बोलने की!
examples.english: How dare you say something like that!

mistakes.why: Would + V1 directly — no "to" with would

mistakes.correct: He dares not go. / He doesn't dare to go.
mistakes.why: 'Dare not' (modal) takes V1; 'doesn't dare' (main verb) takes 'to'

mistakes.wrong: How dare you to say that!

speakingTips: Ultra-polite office requests: 'Would you mind sharing the presentation link?'
Giving moral advice: 'You ought to inform your manager before taking leave.'
Inspirational: 'Dare to dream big — success will follow!'

vocabulary.example: Would you please be more polite?
We ought to fulfill our obligations.
You must dare to show courage.
You ought to be honest in all matters.
You ought to be respectful to elders.
Would you give a genuine opinion?
Do you dare to take on challenging work?
A principled person ought to speak the truth.

practice.hindi: क्या आप please window बंद कर देंगे?
practice.english: Would you please close the window?
alternatives: could you please shut the window?

practice.hindi: क्या आप मुझे एक glass water दे सकते हैं?
practice.english: Would you give me a glass of water?
alternatives: could you get me a glass of water?

practice.hindi: बचपन में वह रोज़ library जाता था।
practice.english: He would go to the library every day as a child.
alternatives: he used to visit the library daily as a child.

practice.hindi: तुम्हें अपने parents को respect करना चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to respect your parents.
alternatives: you should respect your parents.

practice.hindi: वह सच बोलने की हिम्मत रखती है।
practice.english: She dares to speak the truth.
alternatives: she has the courage to tell the truth.

practice.hindi: क्या आप यह form fill करेंगे?
practice.english: Would you fill out this form?
alternatives: could you complete this form?

practice.hindi: तुम्हें समय पर आना चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to come on time.
alternatives: you should arrive on time.

practice.hindi: तुम्हारी यह हिम्मत कैसे हुई ऐसा बोलने की!
practice.english: How dare you say something like that!
alternatives: how dare you speak like that!

practice.hindi: जब वह छोटा था, हर Sunday park जाता था।
practice.english: When he was young, he would go to the park every Sunday.
alternatives: he used to visit the park every sunday when young.

practice.hindi: हम सभी को honest होना चाहिए।
practice.english: We all ought to be honest.
alternatives: we should all be truthful.

practice.hindi: क्या आप please इसे repeat करेंगे?
practice.english: Would you please repeat that?
alternatives: could you please say that again?

practice.hindi: उसने अकेले stage पर जाने की हिम्मत दिखाई।
practice.english: He dared to go on stage alone.
alternatives: he had the courage to perform on stage alone.

practice.hindi: तुम्हें deadline से पहले submit करना चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to submit before the deadline.
alternatives: you should hand in your work before the deadline.

practice.hindi: वह help करता अगर उसके पास time होता।
practice.english: He would help if he had time.
alternatives: he would have helped if he had the time.

practice.hindi: क्या आप इस meeting में join होंगे?
practice.english: Would you join this meeting?
alternatives: will you be part of this meeting?

practice.hindi: तुम्हें अपनी गलती स्वीकार करनी चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to admit your mistake.
alternatives: you should acknowledge your error.

practice.hindi: उसने अकेले उस बड़े company को challenge किया।
practice.english: He dared to challenge that big company alone.
alternatives: he had the boldness to take on that company.

practice.hindi: जब वह student था, हर रात notes बनाता था।
practice.english: When he was a student, he would make notes every night.
alternatives: he used to take notes every night as a student.

practice.hindi: Employees को company rules follow करने चाहिए।
practice.english: Employees ought to follow company rules.
alternatives: workers should adhere to company guidelines.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम please यह document share करोगे?
practice.english: Would you please share this document?
alternatives: could you share this document with me?

practice.hindi: वह नया startup शुरू करने की हिम्मत रखती है।
practice.english: She dares to start a new startup.
alternatives: she has the courage to launch a new company.

practice.hindi: मैं help करता, अगर मेरे पास knowledge होती।
practice.english: I would help if I had the knowledge.
alternatives: i would assist if i knew more about it.

practice.hindi: तुम्हें दूसरों के साथ politely बात करनी चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to speak politely with others.
alternatives: you should be courteous when talking to others.

practice.hindi: तुम्हारी यह हिम्मत कैसे हुई मुझे interrupt करने की!
practice.english: How dare you interrupt me!
alternatives: how dare you cut me off!

practice.hindi: बचपन में वह हर Sunday दादाजी के पास जाती थी।
practice.english: As a child, she would visit her grandfather every Sunday.
alternatives: she used to go to her grandfather every sunday as a child.

practice.hindi: क्या आप कल available होंगे एक call के लिए?
practice.english: Would you be available for a call tomorrow?
alternatives: can we schedule a call tomorrow?

practice.hindi: हमें अपने customers को best service देनी चाहिए।
practice.english: We ought to give our customers the best service.
alternatives: we should provide excellent service to our clients.

practice.hindi: उसने सबके सामने सच बोलने की हिम्मत की।
practice.english: He dared to speak the truth in front of everyone.
alternatives: he was brave enough to tell the truth publicly.

practice.hindi: क्या आप हमारे office visit करेंगे?
practice.english: Would you visit our office?
alternatives: would you be willing to come to our office?

practice.hindi: तुम्हें अपनी health का ख्याल रखना चाहिए।
practice.english: You ought to take care of your health.
alternatives: you should look after your wellbeing.
